channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: INTERPOL ‘Operation Ramz’ seizes 53 malware, phishing servers
item.description: More than 200 individuals were arrested for cybercrime activities during INTERPOL's Operation Ramz, which focused on the Middle East and North Africa. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
item.description: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: SHub macOS infostealer variant spoofs Apple security updates
item.description: A new variant of the 'SHub' macOS infostealer uses AppleScript to show a fake security update message and installs a backdoor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
item.description: The now patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovC...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Spread After Code Release
item.description: The release of Shai-Hulud source code spells trouble for software developers as researchers worry the self-replicating worm could scale....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: Many employees already use shadow AI tools at work without security review. Adaptive Security breaks down how teams can build practical AI governance without adding friction for employees. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Leaked Shai-Hulud malware fuels new npm infostealer campaign
item.description: The Shai-Hulud malware leaked last week is now used in new attacks on the Node Package Manager (npm) index, as infected packages emerged over the weekend. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
item.description: INTERPOL has coordinated a first-of-its-kind cybercrime crackdown across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) that led to 201 arrests and the identification of an additional 382 suspects. The initi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
item.description: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the fami...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Grafana says stolen GitHub token let hackers steal codebase
item.description: Grafana Labs disclosed that hackers have downloaded its source code after breaching its GitHub environment using a stolen access token. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Boring Stuff Is Dangerous Now
item.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt acc...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
item.description: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations,...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft testing adjustable taskbar, Start menu in Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft has finally brought back the resizable taskbar and Start menu to Windows 11 in the latest preview version rolling out to Insiders in the Experimental channel. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
item.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the l...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems
item.description: Chaotic Eclipse, the security researcher behind the recently disclosed Windows flaws, YellowKey and GreenPlasma, has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day flaw ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Four Malicious npm Packages Deliver Infostealers and Phantom Bot DDoS Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered four new npm packages containing information-stealing malware, one of which is a clone of the Shai-Hulud worm open-sourced by TeamPCP. The list of identified ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft confirms Windows 11 security update install issues
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that the May 2026 Windows 11 security update (KB5089549) fails to install on some systems and triggers 0x800f0922 errors. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Exploit available for new DirtyDecrypt Linux root escalation flaw
item.description: A recently patched local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel's rxgk module now has a proof-of-concept exploit that allows attackers to gain root access on some Linux systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
item.description: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. According to Broadcom-owned Symantec and Car...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers earn $1,298,250 for 47 zero-days at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
item.description: The Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 hacking contest has concluded, with security researchers collecting $1,298,250 in rewards after exploiting 47 zero-day flaws. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
item.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Windows 'MiniPlasma' zero-day exploit gives SYSTEM access, PoC released
item.description: A cybersecurity researcher has released a proof-of-concept exploit for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day dubbed "MiniPlasma" that lets attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows s...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
item.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit now supports device-code phishing attacks and abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 Exploited in the Wild, Causing Worker Crashes and Possible RCE
item.description: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. The vulnerability, tracked...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
item.description:
Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase.
"Our investigation has deter...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft rejects critical Azure vulnerability report, no CVE issued
item.description: A security researcher claims Microsoft quietly fixed an Azure Backup for AKS vulnerability after rejecting his report, and without issuing a CVE. Microsoft disputes the claim, telling BleepingComputer...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
